content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all 4 pending amendments for round 17: (1) Added last_proposal_exhaustion_error and last_vote_exhaustion_error to final_review.rs, matching the last_exhaustion_error pattern in completion.rs and prompt_review.rs. The proposal loop early-exit now saves the error via break instead of returning directly, and the post-loop quorum check propagates the saved BackendExhausted error when the shortfall is due to exhaustion. Same pattern applied to the vote loop. (2) Added doc comment on OpenRouter's send_probe documenting that the /models/<id> metadata endpoint is unlikely to surface quota/billing exhaustion — those errors only appear on /chat/completions. Noted as a known limitation with exhaustion detected at invocation time via map_http_error instead. (3) Expanded the compound rate-limit matcher in is_backend_exhausted: improved numeric extraction for 'retry after' to use find+parse instead of trim_start_matches, added 's'/'sec'/'second' suffix matching with a >=60 second threshold (catches 'retry after 3600s', 'retry after 86400 seconds'), and added 'until <digit>' catch-all for time-of-day formats like 'until 9:00 PM UTC'. Added 3 new test functions with 7 new assertions covering large-second values, time-of-day until formats, and transient <60s false-positive prevention. (4) Removed the raw stdout 4KB prefix fallback from all 3 classify_exit_failure_with_output call sites (process_backend.rs ×2, tmux.rs ×1). When extract_stdout_error returns None, empty string is now passed instead of truncated raw stdout. Removed STDOUT_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT constant, unused truncate_str and STDOUT_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT imports from tmux.rs, and dead stdout_text/fresh_stdout_text variables. Updated classify_exit_failure_with_output docstring.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Add last_proposal_exhaustion_error: Option<AppError> to final_review.rs proposal loop. Change the early-exit path from 'return Err(error)' to 'last_proposal_exhaustion_error = Some(error); break'. Update the post-loop quorum check at line ~541 to propagate the saved exhaustion error when the shortfall is due to backend exhaustion, matching the pattern in completion.rs (line ~267) and prompt_review.rs (line ~292).
2. [Completed] Add last_vote_exhaustion_error: Option<AppError> to final_review.rs vote loop. Same pattern: early-exit saves error via break, post-loop quorum check at line ~1150 propagates BackendExhausted error when available.
3. [Completed] Add doc comment on send_probe in openrouter_backend.rs documenting the /models/<id> endpoint limitation: metadata endpoint does not require billing credits, so quota/credit exhaustion errors typically only surface on /chat/completions. Noted as known limitation with exhaustion detected at invocation time via map_http_error.
4. [Completed] Improve 'retry after' numeric extraction in is_backend_exhausted: replaced trim_start_matches with explicit find to locate the end of the numeric portion, then trim_start the remainder. This enables parsing the numeric value for the large-second check while preserving the existing h/hour/day/min/week suffix matching.
5. [Completed] Add large-second detection to 'retry after' heuristic: when suffix starts with 's'/'sec'/'second', parse the numeric portion and classify as persistent if >= 60.0 seconds. Catches 'retry after 3600s', 'retry after 86400 seconds', 'retry after 60 sec'.
6. [Completed] Add 'until <digit>' catch-all to compound rate-limit detection: when 'rate limit' is present and 'until ' is followed by a digit, classify as persistent. Catches time-of-day formats like 'until 9:00 PM UTC', 'until 3:30 AM' that aren't covered by the 'until 2' (ISO timestamp) fixed indicator.
7. [Completed] Add detects_rate_limit_with_retry_after_large_seconds test: 3 assertions for 'retry after 3600s', 'retry after 86400 seconds', 'retry after 60 sec' all detected when combined with 'rate limit'.
8. [Completed] Add detects_rate_limit_with_until_time_of_day test: 2 assertions for 'until 9:00 PM UTC' and 'until 3:30 AM' detected when combined with 'rate limit'.
9. [Completed] Expand no_false_positive_on_retry_after_short_duration test: added assertion that 'retry after 30s' (< 60 seconds) is NOT detected, confirming the threshold works correctly.
10. [Completed] Remove raw stdout fallback from process_backend.rs fresh-retry call site (line ~1454): replaced match with truncate_str fallback with fresh_stdout_error.as_deref().unwrap_or_default(). Removed dead fresh_stdout_text variable. Updated comment explaining the rationale.
11. [Completed] Remove raw stdout fallback from process_backend.rs main failure call site (line ~1493): replaced match with truncate_str fallback with stdout_error.as_deref().unwrap_or_default(). Removed dead stdout_text variable. Updated comment.
12. [Completed] Remove raw stdout fallback from tmux.rs call site (line ~638): replaced match with truncate_str fallback with stdout_error.as_deref().unwrap_or_default(). Removed dead stdout_text variable and unused truncate_str import.
13. [Completed] Remove STDOUT_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT constant from process_backend.rs (no longer used at any call site) and remove its import from tmux.rs.
14. [Completed] Update classify_exit_failure_with_output docstring to reflect the new contract: stdout parameter should contain extracted error text or empty string, not raw stdout.
15. [Completed] Ran cargo fmt to fix formatting differences (test assertion compaction, import line rewrapping). Verified cargo fmt --check passes clean.
16. [Completed] Ran cargo test (754 passed, 0 failed), verified cargo clippy has 0 new warnings in modified files (16 pre-existing in unrelated files), confirmed cargo fmt --check clean.

## Validation Evidence

- All 754 tests pass (cargo test): 570 lib + 166 integration + 1 bench + 754 total, 0 failures, 0 ignored (excluding doc-tests)
- cargo fmt --check passes with no formatting differences
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings produces 0 errors in modified files (final_review.rs, openrouter_backend.rs, process_backend.rs, tmux.rs); all 16 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- final_review.rs now has last_proposal_exhaustion_error and last_vote_exhaustion_error matching completion.rs and prompt_review.rs patterns. Both post-loop quorum checks propagate BackendExhausted when the shortfall is due to exhaustion.
- send_probe doc comment documents the /models/<id> limitation: metadata endpoint unlikely to surface quota exhaustion, detected at invocation time instead
- detects_rate_limit_with_retry_after_large_seconds test passes: 'retry after 3600s', 'retry after 86400 seconds', 'retry after 60 sec' all detected with 'rate limit'
- detects_rate_limit_with_until_time_of_day test passes: 'until 9:00 PM UTC', 'until 3:30 AM' both detected with 'rate limit'
- no_false_positive_on_retry_after_short_duration test passes: 'retry after 1s', 'retry after 100ms', 'retry after 30s' all NOT detected (all < 60 seconds threshold or non-matching suffix)
- All existing rate-limit tests still pass: detects_rate_limit_with_reset_time (compound 'try again at'), detects_rate_limit_with_retry_after_long_duration (h/day/minutes), detects_rate_limit_with_until_timestamp ('until 2026-...'), detects_rate_limit_with_resets_at ('resets at'/'available at'), no_false_positive_on_transient_rate_limit/reached
- STDOUT_EXHAUSTION_SCAN_LIMIT constant removed from process_backend.rs, import removed from tmux.rs. grep confirms no remaining references in the codebase.
- All 3 call sites now pass empty string when extract_stdout_error returns None: process_backend.rs fresh-retry (unwrap_or_default), process_backend.rs main failure (unwrap_or_default), tmux.rs (unwrap_or_default). grep for 'truncate_str.*stdout' in all 3 files confirms no remaining raw-stdout fallback paths.
- All existing exhaustion pattern tests pass: usage limit, quota exceeded, credits exhausted, credits depleted, purchase more credits, insufficient_quota, billing hard limit, hard limit reached
- All panel degradation tests pass: completion (degrade + fail), final_review (degrade + fail), prompt_review (degrade + fail)
- 4 files changed: final_review.rs (last_exhaustion_error propagation in both loops), openrouter_backend.rs (probe limitation doc comment), process_backend.rs (rate-limit matcher expansion + 3 new tests + stdout fallback removal + constant removal + docstring update), tmux.rs (stdout fallback removal + unused import cleanup)

## Outstanding Risks

- Removing the raw stdout fallback means that if a backend prints an exhaustion error as plain text to stdout (not wrapped in a JSON error envelope and not to stderr), it will not be detected as BackendExhausted. This is acceptable because: (a) stderr is always fully scanned, (b) the JSON error envelope path via extract_stdout_error covers all supported CLI backends, and (c) the false-positive risk from model conversation output containing exhaustion keywords was a worse outcome than the false-negative risk from missing plain-text-only stdout errors.
- The 'retry after' large-second heuristic uses a >=60 threshold. A backend that says 'retry after 59s' would be treated as transient. This is a conservative boundary — 59 seconds is borderline, and treating it as transient (retryable) is the safer classification. The threshold can be adjusted if real-world persistent rate limits use values in the 30-59 second range.
- The 'until <digit>' catch-all for time-of-day formats will also match 'until 2...' which overlaps with the existing 'until 2' fixed indicator in RESET_TIME_INDICATORS. This is harmless — the fixed indicator check returns true first, and the 'until <digit>' check only fires if we get past RESET_TIME_INDICATORS without matching (which can't happen for 'until 2...'). The catch-all adds coverage for digits 1, 3-9 (e.g., 'until 9:00 PM').
- The final_review.rs last_exhaustion_error propagation is currently unreachable in practice because the in-loop early-exit always catches the all-exhausted case before the post-loop check fires. The change is purely defensive — it ensures consistent BackendExhausted propagation if future refactoring alters the early-exit logic or loop structure.
